picks.narrative: A 10.2pp gap: IBP has PIT at 53.3%, the market at 43.2%. No individual baseball factor clears the bar for a headline here — this is not a single visible mismatch, it is the model pricing the side differently than the market. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +132 still sits inside the playable range (to +103). The read here is the price, not the profile — the model just makes this a better side than the posted number does.

picks.narrative: The model sees WSH as a 57.3% side; the market is pricing 50.2%, leaving a 6.6pp edge. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.2pp): Carson Palmquist 3.81 xFIP vs Michael Lorenzen 5.15 xFIP. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): COL's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -103 still sits inside the playable range (to -114). The path runs through Carson Palmquist pitching deep enough to keep the game out of the early bullpen.

picks.narrative: IBP makes ATL 63.0% fair versus a 57.4% no-vig market price — a 5.3pp gap. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.9pp), lineup quality (~+0.9pp) — with no single one dominating. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -136 still sits inside the playable range (to -144). As a cluster edge rather than one dominant mismatch, it is a thinner, more fragile signal — worth sizing accordingly.

picks.narrative: IBP values MIL 4.8pp higher than the market — 60.4% model vs 54.8% implied. The largest lift is home field (~+1.0pp): Home-park and 2026 home-game context add a modest edge for MIL. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-0.6pp): Brandon Sproat 4.74 xFIP vs Zac Thornton 4.06 xFIP. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -125 still sits inside the playable range (to -129). The edge is the model pricing MIL differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.

picks.narrative: The model prices MIA at 50.6% to win where the no-vig market implies 46.4%, a 4.5pp disagreement. There's no standout starter, lineup, or bullpen signal driving this — the gap comes from the model's baseline read of the side, not a visible matchup edge. The main pushback is home field (~-0.8pp): HOU's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +117 still sits inside the playable range (to +115). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

picks.narrative: Model and market disagree on MIN by 4.3pp — 46.7% fair against a 42.6% price. No scored baseball driver clears the bar; the standout context is 18 mph wind, which the model's run-environment baseline already reflects. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): CLE's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +136 still sits inside the playable range (to +134). This is largely a price play in a notable run environment — the park/weather context is the backdrop, not a scored matchup edge.

picks.narrative: A 4.2pp gap: IBP has CIN at 52.0%, the market at 47.8%. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+0.8pp), defense (oaa) (~+0.6pp) — with no single one dominating. The main pushback is home field (~-1.1pp): SEA's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road. With a headwind that size, this is not a clean all-signal read. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +109 still sits inside the playable range (to +108). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.
